Container.Name: abed
Container.Overview: Slack and Discord bot for movie/TV show requests with Radarr/Sonarr approval workflow. Users run /movie or /tv commands, select from search results, and approvers can approve or reject requests. Slack uses Socket Mode — no ports required. At least one platform (Slack or Discord) must be configured.
Container.Description: Slack and Discord bot for movie/TV show requests with Radarr/Sonarr approval workflow. Slack uses Socket Mode — no ports required. At least one platform (Slack or Discord) must be configured.

Config.Name: Data Directory
Config.Description: Persistent storage for SQLite database

Config.Name: Slack Bot Token
Config.Description: [Optional if Discord is used] Bot User OAuth Token (starts with xoxb-)

Config.Name: Slack App Token
Config.Description: [Optional if Discord is used] App-Level Token for Socket Mode (starts with xapp-)

Config.Name: Slack Request Channel ID
Config.Description: [Optional if Discord is used] Channel ID where users make requests (starts with C)

Config.Name: Slack Approval Channel ID
Config.Description: [Optional if Discord is used] Channel ID where approval messages appear (starts with C)

Config.Name: Approver Slack IDs
Config.Description: [Optional if Discord is used] Comma-separated Slack user IDs who can approve requests (e.g. U123ABC,U456DEF)

Config.Name: Discord Bot Token
Config.Description: [Optional if Slack is used] Discord Bot Token

Config.Name: Discord Client ID
Config.Description: [Optional if Slack is used] Discord Client ID

Config.Name: Discord Guild ID
Config.Description: [Optional if Slack is used] Discord Server (Guild) ID for slash commands

Config.Name: Discord Request Channel ID
Config.Description: [Optional if Slack is used] Discord Channel ID where users make requests

Config.Name: Discord Approval Channel ID
Config.Description: [Optional if Slack is used] Discord Channel ID where approval messages appear

Config.Name: Approver Discord IDs
Config.Description: [Optional if Slack is used] Comma-separated Discord user IDs who can approve requests

Config.Name: Radarr URL
Config.Description: Radarr base URL (e.g. http://192.168.1.100:7878)

Config.Name: Radarr API Key
Config.Description: Radarr API key from Settings > General

Config.Name: Radarr Quality Profile ID
Config.Description: Numeric ID of the Radarr quality profile (get from /api/v3/qualityProfile)

Config.Name: Radarr Root Folder Path
Config.Description: Root folder path configured in Radarr (e.g. /movies)

Config.Name: DB Path
Config.Description: SQLite database file path inside the container

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID for file permissions

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID for file permissions

Config.Name: Timezone
Config.Description: Container timezone (e.g. America/New_York, Europe/London)

Config.Name: Log Level
Config.Description: Log verbosity: debug | info | warn | error. Set to debug to see HTTP request/response details (secrets are auto-redacted)

Config.Name: Sonarr URL
Config.Description: [Optional — set all 4 Sonarr fields or none] Sonarr base URL (e.g. http://192.168.1.100:8989)

Config.Name: Sonarr API Key
Config.Description: [Optional — set all 4 Sonarr fields or none] Sonarr API key from Settings > General

Config.Name: Sonarr Quality Profile ID
Config.Description: [Optional — set all 4 Sonarr fields or none] Numeric ID of the Sonarr quality profile

Config.Name: Sonarr Root Folder Path
Config.Description: [Optional — set all 4 Sonarr fields or none] Root folder path configured in Sonarr (e.g. /tv)
